llegir 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference
    • Getting started with llegir

Attach a pseudo-bulk ModuleSet view to a cell-level ModuleSet

Source: R/moduleset_pseudobulk.R
with_pseudobulk.Rd

Stores pb_ms on cell_ms and flips cell_ms's pseudobulk capability to TRUE, so cell-level tools keep using cell_ms as their primary view while pseudo-bulk-specific tools pull the sample-level view via pseudobulk() / pseudobulk_view(). Implemented by prepending a decorator class rather than rewriting every adapter: capabilities() and pseudobulk() get an override, every other generic (modules(), gene_membership(), module_scores(), expression(), counts(), metadata(), pkg_versions()) falls through unchanged to cell_ms's own method.

Usage

with_pseudobulk(cell_ms, pb_ms)

# S3 method for class 'with_pseudobulk_ModuleSet'
capabilities(ms, ...)

Arguments

cell_ms

A cell-level ModuleSet (e.g. built by hdWGCNA_ModuleSet()).

pb_ms

A pseudo-bulk ModuleSet, normally built by pseudobulk_ModuleSet().

ms

A ModuleSet object; the dispatch target for capabilities.with_pseudobulk_ModuleSet().

...

Passed to methods.

Value

cell_ms, with pb_ms attached.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
ms <- with_pseudobulk(cell_ms, pb_ms)
has_capability(ms, 'pseudobulk')
pseudobulk(ms)
} # }
